questions.scenario: The Orders API image builds successfully but Cloud Run never marks the revision ready. The app always listens on hardcoded port `3000`. What should the team check?

options.text: Whether the container listens on the port Cloud Run expects through the `PORT` environment variable.
Whether the image tag is longer.
Whether DNS has more TXT records.
Whether the billing account is renamed.

questions.explanation: A built image can still fail the runtime contract if it does not listen where Cloud Run sends traffic.

questions.scenario: The app writes uploaded receipts to the local container filesystem and expects them to be there next week. What is the Cloud Run design issue?

options.text: Container instances are not durable storage homes; durable state should live outside the container.
Every file must be in a Kubernetes Pod.
The service account name is too short.

questions.explanation: Cloud Run instances can be replaced or scaled down. Durable data belongs in a durable service.

questions.scenario: Which items belong in a Cloud Run container contract review?

options.text: Keeping durable state outside the instance filesystem.
Manually SSHing into each Cloud Run instance.

questions.explanation: Cloud Run expects a well-behaved process; it does not expose servers for manual runtime setup.
